title: graders.yaml
description: Composition of L1 (code), L2 (human), and L3 (LLM-as-judge) graders for a recipe. The L1/L2/L3 ladder is from Hamel Husain. The grader-composition pattern is from the Anthropic agent-eval methodology.

min_passk_target.description: Recipe-level minimum acceptable pass^k for the regression suite. Tasks without their own min_passk_target inherit this.

weight.description: Weight in the composed score. The recipe's pass/fail is determined by a weighted sum of grader scores against a threshold.

dimension.description: What this grader is measuring. One dimension per grader (Anthropic discipline) — do not bundle multiple judgements into one grader.

runner.description: Command to invoke. Receives the recipe output on stdin; non-zero exit = fail.

sample_rate.description: Fraction of recipe runs to draw for human review.

queue.description: Path (relative to the recipe's evals/) where annotations land. Typically `annotations/`.

rubric.description: Markdown file describing the rubric the SME applies.
Path (relative to the recipe's evals/) to the markdown rubric. The rubric must instruct the judge to grade ONE dimension and to return `Unknown` when evidence is insufficient (Anthropic guidance).

requires_calibration_within_days.description: Maximum age of the most recent calibration record in calibration.md before this grader is auto-skipped.

max_divergence_from_l2.description: Maximum acceptable disagreement rate between this judge and human L2 ratings. If exceeded at calibration time, this grader is auto-skipped until recalibration brings it back in range.
